ocr.text: The first table presents MIP and source data categorized under Assessable, NSVC, and Total, with Assessable showing values of
599 for MIP, 0 for NSVC, totaling 599; NSVC has values of 6 for MIP, 3 for NSVC, totaling 9; and the Total row indicates 605
for MIP, 3 for NSVC, totaling 608.
MIP and source data  Total
MIP  Assessable  NSVC
Assessable  599  0  599
NSVC  6  3  9
Total  605  3  608
Difference in proportions (95% confidence intervals)  -0.010 (-0.010 to -0.001)
Two-tailed p (McNemar)  0.031

1. Manually abstracted database, # of subjects  2. EHR extract-ion, # of subjects  3. Discrepancy (% and # of subjects) between the databases a  4. Manually abstracted database errors  5. EHR-extracted data errors  6. Median discrepancy  7. Discrepancy range
The difference in proportions is reported as -0.010 with a confidence interval from -0.010 to -0.001, while the two-tailed p value
(McNemar) is 0.031. In the second table, the data covers various parameters with the first WBC count showing 1257 subjects
from the manually abstracted database and 1437 from EHR extraction, presenting a discrepancy of 3.2% (40 subjects), with
manually abstracted database errors at 2.5% (32) and EHR-extracted data errors at 0.6% (8), a median discrepancy of 0.75
k/mm3 and a discrepancy range of 0.01–109 k/mm3. The 1st Hemoglobin counts 1333 and 1460 subjects respectively, with
discrepancies at 11.9% (158), errors of 5.8% (77) and 8.3% (110) respectively, a median of 1.4 g/dl, range 0.1–25.9 g/dl. Peak
total bilirubin has 1565 and 1336 subjects, with 11.4% (152) discrepancy, errors of 6.9% (92) and 5.1% (68), median 1.45
mg/dl, range 0.1–15.2 mg/dl. Peak direct bilirubin has 681 and 674 subjects with 4.9% (33) discrepancy and 4.5% (30) errors
and PDA shows 512 and 414 subjects with 12.8% (53) discrepancies and errors at 12.8% (53).
